title: Fiery Smoky Algerian Chickpeas Tagine
description: A smoky and flavorful Algerian dish featuring chickpeas, lemon, and a blend of spices, with an al-dente finish and a shojin-friendly twist. This meal-prep friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the ground cumin, smoked paprika, and cayenne pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chickpeas, lemon juice, vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue to cook, uncovered, for an additional 10 minutes, or until the chickpeas are tender and the liquid has thickened.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
